current_fact.fact_text: On 23 April 1941, as German troops advanced on Athens, King George II of Greece and his government evacuated the city and eventually went into exile. For Athenians, it was the eerie moment when the ancient capital became a frontline, its classical skyline overshadowed by Second World War turmoil.

episode.title: Morning Drops #11 - Mediterranean Boat
episode.description: Gaia invites you to embark on a musical voyage across the Mediterranean. Exploring music from different countries shared by people who call them home. A warm, sun-soaked journey through coastal sounds.
songs: Fabrizio De André - Crêuza de mä

current_fact.match_reason: A wartime evacuation of Athens connects geographically and emotionally with an episode sailing the Mediterranean, touching Greek shores and the shared sea that has witnessed exile and return.

queue.fact_text: On 23 April 1945, with Berlin surrounded, Hermann Göring sent Hitler a telegram asking whether he should assume leadership of the Reich under a prearranged succession plan. Inside the bunker, Hitler’s circle branded it treason, turning one of history’s bleakest endgames into a paranoid struggle over who would preside over collapse.

episode.title: Morning Drops #49 - Rage Against The Machine
episode.description:
In this Morning Drop, Yasmin revisits the story of Rage Against the Machine — a band born in tension, shaped by conviction, and remembered for moments that blurred the line between stage and protest.
From early Los Angeles unrest to Wall Street performances, this episode moves through rupture, resistance, and legacy — carried by songs that still feel urgent today.
Listen closely.
songs: Rage Against the Machine - Killing in the Name
Rage Against the Machine - Without a Face
Rage Against the Machine - Guerrilla Radio
Rage Against the Machine - Sleep Now in the Fire

queue.match_reason: The crumbling core of a fascist regime fits an episode about political resistance and power structures being challenged, turning history’s darkest politics into fuel for critique.

queue.fact_text: On 23 April 1918, during World War I, the British Royal Navy launched the Zeebrugge Raid, attempting to block the Belgian port of Zeebrugge and trap German U-boats. The operation was only partially successful, but its audacity earned numerous Victoria Crosses and became a symbol of desperate ingenuity at sea.

episode.title: Morning Drops #05 - Electric Shock
episode.description: An energizing episode with electrifying sounds to wake you up and get you moving. High energy tracks that deliver a jolt of musical electricity to start your day.

queue.match_reason: A daring naval assault in stormy seas pairs well with high-voltage rock and blues, echoing the intensity and risk of ships charging into fortified waters.

queue.fact_text: From 23 to 25 April 1993, Eritreans voted in a UN-supervised referendum on independence from Ethiopia, with over 99% choosing statehood. It was the culmination of decades of armed struggle, turning a mountainous corner of the Horn of Africa into one of the world’s newest flags on the map.

episode.title: Morning Drops #24 - The Rise Of Afrobeat
episode.description: An exploration of the rise of Afrobeat, tracing its roots and evolution as a genre that blends African rhythms with jazz, funk, and highlife. A celebration of the infectious energy and cultural significance of this influential musical movement.

queue.match_reason: A referendum on African self-determination fits an episode celebrating Afrobeat’s role in political and cultural liberation across the continent.

queue.fact_text: On 23 April 1990, Namibia joined the United Nations as its 160th member and soon after the Commonwealth, formally ending decades of South African rule and international limbo. For a country defined by vast deserts and a long liberation struggle, it marked an official arrival on the world stage.

episode.title: Morning Drops #23 - Morning Dubs
episode.description: Recorded while still processing Houghton Festival. One of the mornings there, waking up in the woods to a reggae DJ set — no phone signal, just trees, music, and loved ones. A slow selection of reggae, dub, and morning rhythms. Dubs instead of drops. Start your day slow — with space to breathe.
songs: The Gaylads - Joy in the Morning
King Tubby - King Tubby Meets the Rockers Uptown

queue.match_reason: Namibia’s emergence from apartheid‑era control and ‘Babylon’ aligns with roots reggae and dub’s long tradition of singing against colonialism and for African sovereignty.

queue.fact_text: On 23 April 1951, American journalist William N. Oatis was arrested in Czechoslovakia and later sentenced on fabricated espionage charges, becoming a high-profile pawn in early Cold War tensions. His case revealed how reporting and curiosity could be rebranded as spying in a world split by ideology.

episode.title: Morning Drops #27 - Nomophobia
episode.description: Yasmin reflects on what happened when she recently lost her phone and why it felt freeing. A dive into the psychology of our relationship with smartphones: from nomophobia (the fear of being without a phone), to digital burnout and cognitive overload, to the surprising benefits of boredom and the brain's default mode network. An invitation to rethink how we connect and disconnect.

queue.match_reason: A Cold War story about information, surveillance, and control pairs with an episode that questions our modern communication tools and the costs of constant connection.

queue.fact_text: On 23 April 1949, the People’s Liberation Army Navy was formally established, transforming a largely land-focused revolutionary force into a maritime power. From small riverine units, it would grow into one of the world’s largest fleets, reshaping the balance of power across the seas of East Asia.

episode.title: Morning Drops #22 - Sicilians On The Dolomites
episode.description: Hosted by Yasmin, a musical journey inspired by the mountains. Joined by two special guests, exploring the tracks they've chosen, hearing the stories behind their picks, and discovering the personal memories each song brings to life — a blend of music, voices, and landscapes you can hear.
songs: Florian Pellissier Quintet - Coup de foudre à Thessalonique
Lucio Dalla & Francesco De Gregori - Ma come fanno i marinai

queue.match_reason: The birth of a navy connects neatly with an episode that includes "Ma come fanno i marinai"—a song literally about sailors and the emotional pull of the sea.

queue.fact_text: On 23 April 2018, a rental van was deliberately driven onto sidewalks in Toronto, killing 11 people and injuring 15 others along Yonge Street. The attack, carried out in broad daylight, exposed how an everyday vehicle and a busy urban boulevard could be turned into instruments of terror.

episode.title: Morning Drops #42 - The Joy Of Learning
episode.description:
Hosted by Yasmin, this Morning Drop explores learning as a lifelong process, focusing on persistence, repetition, and the emotional landscape that comes with challenging oneself over time.

Through personal stories and scientific insights, the episode looks at how learning reshapes the brain, generates joy through effort, and why progress often arrives after confusion and frustration. It reflects on the so-called delayed pleasure effect, the role of dopamine in motivation, and how curiosity and learning support long-term wellbeing.

The episode also questions our cultural relationship with failure and invites a softer, more realistic approach to learning, one that accepts mistakes as part of growth and sees persistence as a quiet form of strength.

Mood: reflective, grounded, quietly hopeful
Music genre: alternative rock, hip hop, electronic, neo-soul, drill

A space to listen slowly and stay with the process.

queue.match_reason: A difficult, modern urban tragedy is placed alongside a quietly hopeful episode about resilience and long-term growth, offering a gentle counterweight without sensationalism.

queue.fact_text: On 23 April 2005, Jawed Karim uploaded a 19-second clip titled "Me at the zoo," standing in front of elephants at the San Diego Zoo. That unassuming video became the first upload to YouTube, quietly opening the door to a new era where anyone with a camera could broadcast to the world.

episode.title: Morning Drops #54 - Intentional Listening
episode.description:
Do you remember what it felt like to listen to music without a streaming platform?
In this Morning Drops episode, Gaia reflects on a recent experiment: stepping away from algorithmic listening and going back to an iPod. 

What starts as a small personal choice opens into a wider meditation on attention, presence, and what happens when music is no longer treated as something infinite, but as something we choose with care.

Tracklist:
Scientist - Drum Song Dub
The Other People Place - Let Me Be Me
A Forest Mighty Black - Fresh In My Mind
Cymande - Bra
songs: The Other People Place - Let Me Be Me
A Forest Mighty Black - Fresh In My Mind

queue.match_reason: The birth of YouTube, which flooded us with infinite clips, fits an episode questioning algorithmic abundance and arguing for more intentional, finite listening.

queue.fact_text: On 23 April 1993, Sri Lankan politician Lalith Athulathmudali was assassinated while addressing a campaign rally in Colombo, just weeks before provincial elections. His killing deepened the island’s climate of fear, adding another layer of violence to a country already fractured by civil war.

episode.title: Morning Drops #35 - Thailand
episode.description: Recorded in Koh Samui, Thailand, where Yasmin and her friend Patricia spent more than two intense weeks training Muay Thai and Western boxing. Between sweat, rhythm, and sea breeze, they share reflections on discipline, friendship, and the beauty of movement - with a soundtrack that connects Thai sounds to the flow of daily practice.
songs: Burna Boy - Cheat on Me (feat. Dave)

queue.match_reason: A story of political violence in South Asia is balanced with an episode rooted in another part of the region, focused instead on discipline, body, and healing through movement.

queue.fact_text: On 23 April 1985, Coca-Cola announced New Coke, altering its secret formula for the first time in nearly a century. Public backlash was so intense and emotional that the company reversed course in less than three months, turning a marketing misstep into a famous lesson in brand loyalty and nostalgia.

episode.title: Morning Drops #07 - secret_mp3_player_1
episode.description: Inspired by a throwback MP3 player on Instagram, Yasmin and Gaia take you on a nostalgic journey to the early 2000s. LimeWire, eMule, guilty-pleasure playlists — remember those? Hit play and relive the songs that shaped a generation.

queue.match_reason: A story about consumers fiercely defending the taste of their youth pairs with an episode soaked in early‑2000s nostalgia and guilty-pleasure anthems.

queue.fact_text: On 23 April 1971, during the Bangladesh Liberation War, Pakistani forces and local collaborators massacred around 3,000 Hindu refugees in the Jathibhanga area of East Pakistan. Many victims had fled earlier violence, only to be caught and executed in fields that briefly became killing grounds.

episode.title: Morning Drops #16 - The Sound of Tuareg
episode.description: A journey into the sound of the Tuareg: from traditional chants and desert rituals to the electrified rhythms of exile and resistance. Music shaped by sand, memory, and survival.

queue.match_reason: A massacre rooted in identity and displacement resonates with an episode about Tuareg music, where exile, resistance, and memory are braided into desert blues.

queue.fact_text: On 23 April 1968, students at Columbia University in New York City occupied administration buildings to protest the Vietnam War and university ties to defense research and segregation. Their sit‑in and the police response became a defining image of campus rebellion, reshaping how universities and students negotiated power.

episode.title: Morning Drops #08 - (un)Boxing
episode.description: An episode about breaking free from expectations and labels. Music that helps us unbox ourselves and embrace who we truly are.
songs: Kendrick Lamar - The Blacker the Berry

queue.match_reason: Student occupations against war and racism match an episode about unboxing identity and challenging the systems and labels that confine us.

queue.fact_text: On 23 April 1966, Aeroflot Flight 2723 crashed into the Caspian Sea off the Absheron Peninsula, killing all 33 people on board. The aircraft vanished from radar in poor weather, and its loss added to the long, often opaque record of Soviet-era aviation disasters.

episode.title: Morning Drops #33 - Immerse
episode.description: An analogy between diving, one of Gaia's great passions, and the act of immersing in life itself. A reflection on what happens when we go below the surface: when we let ourselves be fully present. From moments of calm suspension to warm, rhythmic flow, the selection moves like water — fluid, organic, and alive. An invitation to slow down, listen deeper, and float.
songs: Pino D'Angiò - Un Po' d'Uva e un Liquore

queue.match_reason: A plane disappearing into the Caspian Sea is paired with an episode themed around immersion and water—transforming a story of sinking into one about floating and presence.

queue.fact_text: On 23 April 1999, during the NATO bombing campaign against Yugoslavia, warplanes struck the headquarters of Radio Television of Serbia in Belgrade, killing 16 media workers. Targeting a broadcaster as a "propaganda" facility ignited fierce debate about the boundaries between information, influence, and civilian life in modern warfare.

episode.title: Sedna FM #01 - November Bomb
episode.description: The first Family & Friends radio episode from Yasmin Sarbaoui and Gaia Parolini. Where it all began — a life soundtrack that launched Sedna FM into the universe. Raw, heartfelt, and full of the energy that defines everything that followed.
songs: Canserbero - De la vida como pelicula, tragedia, comedia y ficcion

queue.match_reason: A bombing of a TV and radio headquarters is hauntingly mirrored by Sedna’s own debut, "November Bomb"—a peaceful ‘bomb’ of sound that reclaims broadcasting for community and creativity.

queue.fact_text: On 23 April 1967, the Soyuz 1 mission that had launched so hopefully ended in tragedy when Vladimir Komarov’s capsule crashed on reentry after its parachutes failed to deploy. He is often described as the first human to die during a spaceflight, and his story is a stark reminder that exploration and risk are inseparable.

episode.title: Morning Drops #14 - Slow Planet
episode.description: A pause. A quiet return to slower rhythms, sparked by a live set under the summer sky. A moment to breathe and settle into the week.

queue.match_reason: The somber end of a rushed space mission is set against an episode advocating slowness and care—a ‘slow planet’ in response to the dangers of pushing too fast.

queue.fact_text: On 23 April 1967, Soyuz 1’s problems began almost immediately after launch, with a solar panel failing to deploy and the spacecraft tumbling in orbit. Komarov spent orbits wrestling with malfunctioning controls, making him not only a cosmonaut but an impromptu test pilot for a spacecraft that wasn’t ready.

episode.title: Sedna FM #06 - Alters of an Ego
episode.description: What if you could step outside yourself and become someone freer, bolder, or completely unexpected? Exploring the artistry of musicians who craft alter-egos and parallel realities to experiment with new dimensions of their identity. It's not about hiding who they are — it's about expanding who they can be.
songs: The Fireman - Strawberries Oceans Ships Forest

queue.match_reason: A cosmonaut improvising in a barely-tested craft feels like a human forced into an unintended role—echoing this episode’s focus on alternate selves and improvising new identities under pressure.

queue.fact_text: On 23 April 1967, as ground controllers realized Soyuz 1 was in serious trouble, engineers frantically considered launching a second Soyuz to rescue Komarov—an idea ultimately abandoned as too risky. The almost‑rescue that never happened adds an eerie layer to a mission already heavy with what‑ifs.

episode.title: Morning Drops #43 - Looking Back
episode.description:
In this first Morning Drop of 2026, Yasmin opens a space for quiet reflection on the nature of transitions. The episode traces a personal journey that began three years ago, when a sudden injury on New Year’s Day turned a desire for movement into a mandatory season of stillness.

Through the story of a broken knee and the slow process of relearning to walk, the narrative explores the difference between being strong and being aware. It touches on the vulnerability of the body, the unexpected joy found in daily progress, and the warmth that comes from finally allowing oneself to accept help. The musical selection mirrors this path of recovery, moving from the uncertainty of change to the steady rhythm of healing.

Mood: Reflective, Resilient, Grounded
Music genre: Indie Pop, Cinematic, World, Soul

We invite you to listen deeply and find comfort in your own pace.
songs: Kelly Finnigan - I Don't Wanna Wait
Goran Bregović & Iggy Pop - This Is a Film

queue.match_reason: An abandoned rescue plan and the limits of what can be fixed echo an episode about injury, recovery, and learning to accept the boundaries of the body and of time.

queue.fact_text: On 23 April 1967, the broader Soviet space program that launched Soyuz 1 was racing the United States toward ever more ambitious milestones, often under immense political pressure. Komarov’s doomed flight revealed how human lives were entangled with a system that treated space as both frontier and propaganda stage.

episode.title: Sedna FM #02 - Destination Sedna
episode.description: Continuing the Sedna FM journey together with family and friends! Episode two of the main series — exploring new musical territories and deepening the connection with our listeners. A collective voyage through sound.
songs: Odion Iruoje - Identify With Your Roots
Depeche mode - world in my eyes (Oil Tank Mix)

queue.match_reason: A story about a superpower’s cosmic ambitions and propaganda fits an episode framed as a ‘Destination’ journey through spacey sounds and new territories, but on Sedna’s own terms.

published.fact_text: On 23 April 1967, the Soviet Union launched Soyuz 1, the first crewed mission of its new Soyuz spacecraft, carrying cosmonaut Vladimir Komarov into orbit. The flight would become infamous for a cascade of technical failures that Komarov calmly reported back to Earth, turning a triumph of engineering into one of space exploration’s most sobering cautionary tales.

episode.title: Morning Drops #03 - Space Shift
episode.description: Gaia invites you to shift your mindset with a simple yet powerful reflection: What if we saw emptiness not as a void but as space to create? An energising and feel-good selection of tracks, the perfect way to ease into your day and set the tone for the week ahead.
songs: Willy J Healey - Woke Up Smiling
The Strokes - You Only Live Once

published.match_reason: A story from the early space age pairs naturally with an episode themed around ‘Space Shift’, turning orbital emptiness into a creative, forward-moving mindset.

published.fact_text: On 23 April 1909, a magnitude 6.0 earthquake struck near Lisbon, shaking the Ribatejo region and devastating the town of Benavente. The quake and its soil liquefaction turned streets into mud and claimed at least 60 lives, reminding Portugal that the ground beneath its capital is still restless centuries after the great 1755 quake.

episode.title: Morning Drops #45 - It Can't Rain Forever
episode.description:
In this Morning Drop, Gaia returns from the vivid landscapes of Morocco to open a space for reflection. This episode navigates the delicate transition between closing a significant chapter and stepping into the new year. It explores the concept of constancy amidst change, celebrating the grounding force of friendship and the creative sanctuary that is Sedna FM.

The musical selection mirrors this journey, moving from the orchestral depth of changing seasons into the golden warmth of psychedelic soul. It recalls memories of Swiss mountains and unexpected downpours, reminding us that shared optimism can weather any storm.

Take a moment to breathe in the new morning and listen deeply.

Mood: Reflective, Warm, Transitional, Hopeful
Music genre: Classic Rock, Psychedelic Soul, Pop Rock, Soft Rock

published.match_reason: A dramatic earthquake story fits a reflective, weather‑themed episode about storms, resilience, and the ground eventually settling again.

published.fact_text: On 23 April 1724, Johann Sebastian Bach premiered his cantata "Du Hirte Israel, höre" (BWV 104) in Leipzig, a pastoral work that paints the image of a divine shepherd with gently flowing melodies and intricate choral writing. It’s a snapshot of how Baroque music turned theology and countryside imagery into a surprisingly intimate soundscape.

episode.title: Morning Drops #40 - Diving With Orcas
episode.description: Yasmin's friend Caroline takes us beyond the polar circle, into the Arctic waters of Norway, where orcas and humpback whales gather each winter. A personal journey shaped by a quiet obsession with orcas, from Patagonia to the icy fjords of the north. The soundscape moves gently between cinematic swells and soft, rhythmic pulses — echoing the cold light of Arctic mornings and the grace of underwater encounters.

published.match_reason: Bach’s pastoral, sacred imagery resonates with this cinematic, nature‑driven episode that treats the ocean as a vast, almost spiritual landscape.

published.fact_text: On 23 April 1500, Portuguese navigator Pedro Álvares Cabral sighted the shores of what is now Brazil, claiming the land for Portugal while officially en route to India. His fleet, pushed westward by Atlantic currents and winds, turned an intended detour around Africa into the beginning of a new chapter in South American history.

episode.title: Morning Drops #31 - Sailing With Germans
episode.description: From the deck of a sailing boat, hosted by Yasmin and her special guests. A week at sea with six Germans, each with their own stories, songs, and ways of moving through the waves. From shared meals to unexpected laughter, from quiet resilience to sun-soaked joy — a collection of voices and tracks that reflect what it means to journey together.
songs: Ben Howard - Keep Your Head Up

published.match_reason: An Age of Discovery sea voyage aligns perfectly with an episode literally recorded on a boat, celebrating long journeys, currents, and shared horizons.

published.fact_text: On 23 April 215 BC (by Roman reckoning), the Romans vowed and later built a temple to Venus Erycina on the Capitoline Hill after a crushing defeat by Hannibal at Lake Trasimene. Instead of honoring a victory, this sanctuary marked a moment of collective anxiety, asking a love and fortune goddess to watch over a shaken republic.

episode.title: Morning Drops #38 - History of Flamenco
episode.description: A special journey into the history of Flamenco, guided by Yasmin and her guest Almudena from Madrid, Spain. Exploring the roots and evolution of this emotionally raw and culturally rich tradition, from its early vocal origins and Andalusian influences, through its transformation on stage and screen, to its modern reinventions by iconic Spanish artists.
songs: La Niña de los Peines - Al Gurugú
Paco de Lucía - Entre dos aguas
Camarón de la Isla - La leyenda del tiempo

published.match_reason: An ancient Roman temple to a love/fate goddess fits the intense, ritualistic emotion of flamenco, another Mediterranean art born from collective pain and devotion.

published.fact_text: On 23 April 1941, as German troops advanced on Athens, King George II of Greece and his government evacuated the city and eventually went into exile. For Athenians, it was the eerie moment when the ancient capital became a frontline, its classical skyline overshadowed by Second World War turmoil.

episode.title: Morning Drops #11 - Mediterranean Boat
episode.description: Gaia invites you to embark on a musical voyage across the Mediterranean. Exploring music from different countries shared by people who call them home. A warm, sun-soaked journey through coastal sounds.
songs: Fabrizio De André - Crêuza de mä

published.match_reason: A wartime evacuation of Athens connects geographically and emotionally with an episode sailing the Mediterranean, touching Greek shores and the shared sea that has witnessed exile and return.
